title: orthochilus aculeatus subsp. huttonii
intro: Orthochilus aculeatus subsp. huttonii is a species of orchid commonly known as the Hutton's Orthochilus. It is a flowering plant found in New Zealand, known for its medicinal properties and health benefits. The plant is traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The Hutton's Orthochilus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal and therapeutic uses.
